From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Lammermuir (clipper)](/w/index.php?title=Lammermuir_(clipper)&redirect=no "Lammermuir (clipper)"))

British Vessel

For other ships with the same name, see [Lammermuir (1856 clipper)](/wiki/Lammermuir_(1856_clipper) "Lammermuir (1856 clipper)").

| --- | --- |
|  | |
| *Lammermuir*, built in 1864 | |
|  | |

| Name | *Lammermuir* |
| Namesake | [Lammermuir Hills](/wiki/Lammermuir_Hills "Lammermuir Hills") |
| Owner | [J&R Dunbar Willis](/wiki/Jock_Willis_Shipping_Line "Jock Willis Shipping Line") |
| Port of registry | [London](/wiki/Port_of_London "Port of London") |

| Yard number | 60 |

|  | |

***Lammermuir*** was an extreme [clipper](/wiki/Clipper "Clipper") ship built in 1864 by [Pile](/wiki/William_Pile_(shipbuilder) "William Pile (shipbuilder)"), Spence and Company of [West Hartlepool](/wiki/West_Hartlepool "West Hartlepool") for [John "Jock" "White Hat" Willis & Son](/wiki/Jock_Willis_Shipping_Line "Jock Willis Shipping Line"), [London](/wiki/London "London"). She was the second ship to bear the name. The first [*Lammermuir*](/wiki/Lammermuir_(1856_clipper) "Lammermuir (1856 clipper)") had been the favorite ship of John Willis, and was wrecked in the [Gaspar Strait](/wiki/Gaspar_Strait "Gaspar Strait") in 1863.

## Building

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lammermuir_(1864_clipper)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Building")]

*Lammermuir* was built at Swanson Dock in West Hartlepool, launching her on 23 July 1864 and completing her on 2 February 1865.[[1]](#cite_note-WBS-1) She had an iron hull. Her registered length was 200.4 ft (61.1 m), her beam was 35.5 ft (10.8 m), her depth was 20.9 ft (6.4 m) and her [tonnage](/wiki/Tonnage "Tonnage") was 1,054 [NRT](/wiki/Net_register_tonnage "Net register tonnage").[[2]](#cite_note-2) She had three masts and was a [full-rigged ship](/wiki/Full-rigged_ship "Full-rigged ship").

Willis [registered](/wiki/Ship_registration "Ship registration") the ship at [London](/wiki/Port_of_London "Port of London"). Her UK [official number](/wiki/Official_number "Official number") 50192 was and her [code letters](/wiki/Code_letters "Code letters") were HCVW.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lammermuir_(1864_clipper)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

*Lammermuir* was designed for the China [tea](/wiki/Tea "Tea") trade. In 1866 she was almost wrecked in the [East China Sea](/wiki/East_China_Sea "East China Sea") and the [Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean") by two [typhoons](/wiki/Typhoon "Typhoon"). Her [Master](/wiki/Master_mariner "Master mariner") was [Captain](/wiki/Sea_captain "Sea captain") M Bell, and she carried the famous [Lammermuir Party](/wiki/Lammermuir_Party "Lammermuir Party") of 18 missionaries and four children of the [China Inland Mission](/wiki/OMF_International "OMF International") outbound to China, arriving in [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai") on 30 September 1866.

[Hudson Taylor](/wiki/Hudson_Taylor "Hudson Taylor") recalled the most perilous time in the voyage:

> The appearance of things was now truly terrific. Rolling fearfully, the [masts](/wiki/Mast_(sailing) "Mast (sailing)") and [yards](/wiki/Yard_(sailing) "Yard (sailing)") hanging down were tearing our only [sail](/wiki/Sail "Sail")... and battering like a ram against the [main yard](/wiki/Main_yard "Main yard"). The deck from [forecastle](/wiki/Forecastle "Forecastle") to [poop](/wiki/Poop_deck "Poop deck") was one scarcely broken sea. The roar of the water, the clanging of chains, the beating of the dangling masts and yards, the sharp smack of the torn sails made it almost impossible to hear any orders that might be given.

In 1873 *Lammermuir* left London for [Adelaide](/wiki/Adelaide "Adelaide"), but without the all-important tool chest for her ship's carpenter. John Willis himself rushed to the docks to see to it that *Orient* could take the chest along and deliver it to the ship. The captain of *Orient* bet Willis that he would overtake *Lammermuir* before it crossed the Equator to transfer the chest at sea, which he did.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The clipper's last voyage was soon after this, from Adelaide to London on 10 November 1876. She never arrived, and was presumed to have been lost at sea.[[1]](#cite_note-WBS-1)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lammermuir_(1864_clipper)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: References")]

2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** [*Lloyd's Register of British and Foreign Shipping*](https://archive.org/details/HECROS1866/page/n458/mode/1up). [Lloyd's Register](/wiki/Lloyd%27s_Register "Lloyd's Register"). 1866. LAM. Retrieved 30 May 2022 – via [Internet Archive](/wiki/Internet_Archive "Internet Archive").
3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** [*Mercantile Navy List*](https://www.crewlist.org.uk/data/viewimages?year=1871&name=Lammermuir&page=280&steamsail=Sail&gotopage=next). 1871. p. 199. Retrieved 30 May 2022 – via Crew List Index Project.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** Lubbock, 1921, p. 151
